title: PV Sindhu's Early Steps to Stardom in Macau
indian_angle: This feature highlights the formative years of Indian badminton icon PV Sindhu, connecting her journey to a unique location.
summary: A look back at the beginnings of PV Sindhu's illustrious career, tracing her initial steps towards becoming a global badminton star in Macau.

title: HS Prannoy Aims for Strong Comeback After Tough 2023 Season
indian_angle: Indian star HS Prannoy shares his aspirations for a strong comeback, focusing on improving his game and mentoring young players.
summary: Indian shuttler HS Prannoy expresses confidence in replicating his past successes, focusing on game improvement and supporting the next generation of Indian badminton talent.

title: BAI Warns Public About Unauthorized Badminton Premier League
indian_angle: The national governing body, BAI, is issuing an advisory to protect players and the public from unapproved domestic tournaments.
summary: Badminton Association of India issues an advisory regarding an unauthorized 'Badminton Premier League – Season 2', urging caution to players and stakeholders.

title: Grassroot Coaches Program Scheduled for July in Guwahati, Raipur
indian_angle: This program aims to strengthen the foundational coaching infrastructure across India, crucial for developing future talent.
summary: BAI announces a Coaches Development Program for the grassroot level, to be held in Guwahati and Raipur from July 15th to 24th, 2026.

title: Chennai to Host All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament
indian_angle: This is a key domestic tournament for Indian senior players to compete and improve their national rankings.
summary: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 is scheduled to take place in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16th to 23rd.

title: Thomas Cup Squad Members Return for Asian Games Prep
indian_angle: Key Indian players, including Treesa and Gayatri, are regrouping from the successful Thomas Cup squad to prepare for the upcoming Asian Games.
summary: Members of India's historic Thomas Cup winning squad, including the women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, are reassembling for preparations for the Asian Games.
